# Problem Definition -- Reasoning

## Why This Section Matters

The problem definition is the foundation of the entire venture thesis. Without a clear, validated problem statement, all downstream analysis (market, solution, business model) rests on assumptions.

## Key Claims & Assumptions

### High Confidence
- The problem is wicked (8/10 characteristics identified)
- TAM estimate 2-5B based on analogous market data

### Medium Confidence
- Regulatory headwinds in EU may slow adoption

### Low Confidence
- China market timing remains speculative

## Minto Structure

### Situation
Venture intelligence tools exist but treat each analysis dimension in isolation.

### Complication
Cross-section reasoning (how problem definition affects financial model) is invisible to current tools.

### Question
Can a structured reasoning engine make cross-section logic visible and persistent?

### Answer
Yes -- by capturing reasoning as artifacts with dependency graphs, future sessions understand WHY each section looks the way it does.

## Cross-Section Logic

Problem definition INFORMS market-analysis (customer pain validation) and AFFECTS solution-design (solution must address wicked characteristics) and competitive-analysis (competitors who simplify the problem miss the wickedness).

## What Larry Would Challenge

Is the problem truly wicked, or are we conflating complexity with wickedness? The 8/10 score needs scrutiny -- which 2 characteristics are missing and why?

## Verification Criteria

- Problem definition cites at least 2 customer data points
- Wicked characteristics identified and scored
